title: Word of God
description: The creative and revelatory speech of God. By his word the heavens were made; by his word the prophets spoke; in the Word made flesh God's fullest self-disclosure arrived. Scripture is the written form of this living, active word.

stops.label: God Said, 'Let There Be Light'
stops.development: The first recorded speech of God. Creation happens through divine speech: God says, and reality conforms.
stops.what_changes: The word of God is established as performative and creative. What God speaks, exists. Language and reality are linked at the deepest level.

stops.label: Man Lives by Every Word
stops.development: Moses reminds Israel: God humbled them with manna 'that he might make you know that man does not live by bread alone, but by every word from the mouth of the LORD.'
stops.what_changes: The word of God is established as life-sustaining. Physical provision is secondary to the sustenance of divine speech.

stops.label: The Still Small Voice
stops.development: God is not in the wind, earthquake, or fire, but in the 'still small voice.' Elijah wraps his face and listens.
stops.what_changes: The word of God is shown to come in unexpected modes. Divine communication need not be spectacular to be authoritative.

stops.label: My Word Shall Not Return Empty
stops.development: God's word goes out and accomplishes its purpose, like rain that waters the earth and makes it bring forth and sprout.
stops.what_changes: The word of God is declared effective and irrevocable. It cannot fail to accomplish what it was sent to do.

stops.label: Fire and Hammer
stops.development: 'Is not my word like fire, declares the LORD, and like a hammer that breaks the rock in pieces?'
stops.what_changes: The word of God has destructive and transformative power. It cannot be domesticated or made comfortable.

stops.label: The Word Was God
stops.development: 'In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.' The logos of Genesis 1 is a person.
stops.what_changes: The word of God is revealed as personal and divine. The concept that began as divine speech becomes incarnate in Christ.

stops.label: Living and Active
stops.development: The word of God is 'living and active, sharper than any two-edged sword, piercing to the division of soul and spirit.'
stops.what_changes: The word of God penetrates and judges the inner person. It is not merely informational but transformationally incisive.

stops.label: His Name Is the Word of God
stops.development: The returning Christ is named 'The Word of God.' The creative word of Genesis, the prophetic word of the OT, and the incarnate Word of John 1 converge.
stops.what_changes: The word of God reaches its eschatological climax. The one who spoke creation into being returns to speak the final word over all creation.
